name: machine_activation_sdk_app_integration_spec
purpose: Guide an agent or tool to port an existing AI-native app from cloud inference to the Machine Activation SDK while preserving the app UI and minimizing product friction.
primary_goal: Keep the app's existing UI and product behavior, replace the inference layer with Machine Activation SDK integration, and preserve a simple cartridge-style user flow: pick/import model, save config, activate session, run inference.
non_goals: Do not redesign the app UI unless needed for activation setup or local/cloud proof.
Do not turn diagnostics into default activation blockers.
Do not force backend-specific quirks into the generic SDK surface unless they are truly reusable.

required_inputs.description: Absolute path to the app being ported.
Target platforms for local inference, for example android, ios, react-native, capacitor, web.
Files/services where the app currently calls cloud inference.
Backend/runtime lane to use first, for example llama.cpp, llama.rn, LiteRT, LiteRT-LM.

porting_workflow.description: Find the current cloud analysis and chat call sites, validation detours, and any hidden fallback paths.
Add a simple provider seam such as cloud vs machine_activation without changing the app's visible product flow more than necessary.
Register the app's local runtime adapter so the Machine Activation SDK can create sessions.
Use the SDK's stored model-config concepts instead of app-specific raw-path state when possible.
Prefer the SDK's reusable setup flow for load, pick/import, optional verify, save, and clear instead of rebuilding picker logic from scratch.
Create the smallest acceptable user flow for model selection: pick/import, save config, activate on demand.
Swap the app's main analysis path to Machine Activation when local mode is active.
Swap follow-up chat to Machine Activation when the app includes a product chat surface.
Eliminate validation or fallback routes that still quietly hit cloud inference when local mode is selected.
Add a shaping layer between raw local model output and the app UI so missing arrays or nested fields do not crash the product.
Show a small visible local/cloud source indicator during processing and or results.
Record runtime-specific details separately from generic SDK promises.

success_criteria: The app keeps its existing UI and product identity.
The app can run analysis through Machine Activation when local mode is selected.
The app can run chat through Machine Activation when applicable.
The user can select or import a local model without expert-only friction.
Diagnostics do not block activation except for impossible cases.
The app visibly indicates local vs cloud inference source.
The integration is documented well enough for a future agent to repeat the pattern.

notes.backend_specific_runtime_notes: Keep runtime quirks such as LiteRT-LM image invocation details in backend-specific notes, not in the generic activation promise.
notes.strict_mode_guidance: Strict compatibility policy should be opt-in for apps that explicitly want hard preflight enforcement.
notes.ui_guidance: A subtle source badge is preferred over loud product chrome.
notes.backend_strategy: Prefer llama-family runtimes for GGUF cartridges and LiteRT-family runtimes for LiteRT package formats. The SDK should focus on activation and integration, not on duplicating mature backend capabilities.
